summary.intent: Implement task #280: a framework-agnostic Calendar machine (single + multi-select, locale-aware grid, min/max constraints, focus management, ARIA announcements) for the ars-ui Rust component library.
summary.outcome: Calendar machine fully implemented and merged to PR #688 with 157+ tests, 6 Codex review passes addressed, spec kept in sync with all implementation deviations documented, and all CI gates green.

repo: CalendarDate does not implement Ord — comparisons must use .compare() method; BTreeSet<CalendarDate> is unusable, requiring a SelectedDates sorted-Vec newtype.
ars-i18n real API uses u8 + Result<_, CalendarError> for date construction and arithmetic, not the NonZero<u8> + infallible API described in the spec.
Callback<dyn Fn(Args) -> Out> requires Args: 'static, making it incompatible with &T reference arguments out of the box; required adding a new_ref constructor.
Tests use drop(svc.send(...)) not let _ = svc.send(...) — clippy enforces this distinction.
ConnectApi is accessed via svc.connect not Machine::connect.
ComponentIds replaces raw heading_id/grid_id string fields for part ID derivation.
cargo xci-fast is the mandatory pre-push gate (10 steps); must pass before every push.
cargo insta review accepts all pending snapshots workspace-wide — unrelated snapshots (e.g., data_display) get caught and must be manually reverted after acceptance.

Codex auto-triggers on push (shows 👀 immediately); posting a manual @codex review when 👀 is already present queues a duplicate review against the same commit, producing stale duplicate finding threads.
proptest_config() is the required harness setup for proptest_state_machines tests.
Button elements in connect attrs must set type=button explicitly or they default to submit and trigger parent form submission.

code.finding: Callback::new requires Args: 'static, blocking use with &T reference args. Added new_ref constructor using HRTB (for<'a> Fn(&'a T) -> Out) to support predicate callbacks over references.

month_step_plan must be atomic: try focused_date shift first, abort (return None) if it fails, then advance visible_month/year only on success. Otherwise visible and focused state diverge on calendar boundaries.
sync_visible_to_focused must be called after any focused_date clamp (in sync_props_into_ctx, SetMonth, SetYear, and paging) or the view renders the wrong month with no cell at tabindex=0.
FocusOut and SyncProps events must bypass the ctx.disabled guard — only interaction events should be blocked when disabled, not focus lifecycle or prop sync events.
is_prev_disabled / is_next_disabled must account for page step size (visible_months * page_behavior step) and return true when post-step date construction fails at calendar boundaries.

Unavailable cells must NOT receive HTML disabled attribute — only aria-disabled. HTML disabled removes the cell from the focus model entirely, violating the spec (unavailable dates are focusable, not selectable).
weeks_for must return an empty Vec (not a partial result) when add_days fails near calendar boundaries — the spec contract is always 6 complete rows or nothing.

Part::Cell and Part::CellTrigger need an offset: usize field so multi-month consumers using generic part_attrs correctly route to the right grid offset rather than always using the first visible month.
aria-label suffixes '(unavailable)' and '(disabled)' must be routed through the Messages struct, not hardcoded English strings.

workflow: TDD discipline: write failing regression tests for every Codex finding before implementing the fix — prevents re-regression and proves the fix targets the right code path.
Batch all fixes for a Codex pass into a single commit+push, then resolve all threads atomically — avoids Codex reviewing intermediate states and generating duplicate stale findings.
Check for 👀 reaction before posting @codex review; auto-trigger on push makes manual trigger redundant and produces duplicate review queues.
When accepting insta snapshots after a fix, filter to only the target component's snapshots to avoid inadvertently accepting unrelated pre-existing failures.
Spec and implementation changes must ship in the same commit — keeping them in sync within the PR prevents spec drift and makes the PR self-documenting.
Mutation tests (cargo mutants) are unreliable in this repo due to pre-existing baseline failures; use --baseline=skip and treat partial runs as signal rather than ground truth.

friction: Callback type's Args: 'static constraint blocked use with &CalendarDate reference predicates — required adding a new_ref constructor to ars-core (unplanned ars-core change).
CalendarDate lacking Ord forced a SelectedDates newtype workaround and manual comparison throughout, despite BTreeSet being the natural data structure for a date set.
Codex reviewing against pre-push commits generated 3–4 stale duplicate finding threads per pass, adding noise and requiring manual identification of dups vs new findings.
cargo insta review accepted data_display and meter snapshots that had pre-existing failures — required manual revert after each acceptance run.
cargo mutants ran for 15+ minutes and timed out or produced ambiguous output multiple times; had to be killed and treated as incomplete signal.
The spec used NonZero<u8> + infallible CalendarDate API that doesn't match real ars-i18n — required discovering the real API surface through source reading rather than spec trust.
Manual PartialEq impl was required for Props when using fn pointer (before Callback migration) due to clippy fn-pointer comparison warning.

SetMonth and SetYear required multiple fix iterations: first for atomicity (don't write visible state before focused_date succeeds), then again for sync_visible_to_focused after clamp — each discovered in a separate Codex pass.
open_items: §5.5 multi-select keyboard extras deferred: Shift+Space range-from-anchor and Ctrl+A select-all require an undefined 'anchor' concept not yet in the spec — needs spec design before implementation.
Hover events (Event::HoverDate, Event::HoverEnd), Context.is_range/range_start/range_end, and on_cell_hover/on_grid_mouseleave are deferred to a future RangeCalendar component — noted in PR body.
Callback::new_ref is now available in ars-core but other components with fn-pointer predicate Props (similar to is_date_unavailable) could adopt it — no tracking issue created.
Mutation test coverage not definitively established due to tooling instability; two partial runs showed 0 MISSED but a complete run was never confirmed.
Shift+Space anchor-range and Ctrl+A select-all noted in tests as explicit TODOs but not test-gated — if spec adds anchor concept, implementation will be needed.
